title: PHYSECON
PHYSECON
PHYSECON
text:
2 Potential
\(\Rightarrow\) Wave equation that determines Potential: \[\begin{split} &
\Delta \cdot U - \frac{1}{c^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} U = J
\end{split} \tag{6}\]
\(\Rightarrow\) Potential caused by “ignition J” (inhomgenity) from source at rest, v = 0: \[\begin{split} &
U = -\frac{1}{4\pi} \int \frac{J}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\end{split} \tag{7}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
3 elec. Potential structure:
\(J = -\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \varrho\) \[\begin{split} &
U_e \coloneqq \Phi
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. charge dens.=}\varrho}{=}
\frac{1}{4\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \int \frac{\varrho}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} Q =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} It
\end{split} \tag{8}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
4 magn. Potential structure:
\(J = -\mu_0 j\) \[\begin{split} &
U_m \coloneqq \boldsymbol{A}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. current dens.=}j}{=}
\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \int \frac{j}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \mu_0 I =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} I =
\frac{1}{c^2} \frac{1}{t} U_e
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{source in uniformal movement,} v = const. \neq 0}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \mu_0 Qv
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} vQ
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Itv =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Ir
\end{split} \tag{9}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
5 Electric endogen energy

Bond Energy
Binding Energy

\(E_{pot} = mc^2\)
\[\begin{split} &
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
q = ne = \textnormal{charge} \qquad
n \in \mathbb{N}
\end{split} \tag{10}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\textnormal{Potential} \qquad
U =
\frac{E_{pot}}{q} =
\frac{\left( mc^2 \right)^2}{ne} =
\frac{m}{q} m c^4 =
\kappa m c^4
\end{split} \tag{11}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
6 Electric induced energy (exogenous excitation)

Ionisation Energy
Exitation Energy

\(E_{kin} = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \> \hat{=} \> \left( pc \right)^2 \> \hat{=} \> h\nu\)
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density electric: \[\begin{split} &
w_e = \frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E}^2
\end{split} \tag{12}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density magnetic: \[\begin{split} &
w_m = \frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B}^2
\end{split} \tag{13}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Electric Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_e = \frac{1}{2} CU^2
\end{split} \tag{14}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Magnetic Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_m = \frac{1}{2} LI^2
\end{split} \tag{15}\]

6.1 Power:
\[\begin{split} &
P = VI = (U_1 - U_2) \frac{Q}{t} =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{t} = \frac{W}{t} = \frac{Fr}{t} = Fv
\end{split} \tag{16}\]


6.2 Frequency:
\[\begin{split} &
\omega = \dot{\rho} = \frac{v}{r} = 2 \pi \nu = k \lambda \frac{1}{T}
\stackrel{vacuum}{=}
kc
\newline &
\stackrel{matter}{=}
k \frac{c}{\eta} = kc \frac{\tan\beta}{\tan\alpha}
\> \hat{=} \>
k c \left( \mathscr{E_\beta}^{-1} \mathscr{E_\alpha} \right)^{-1} =
k c \frac{\varepsilon_\beta}{\varepsilon_\alpha} =
k c \sqrt{\frac{\varepsilon_0\mu_0}{\varepsilon\mu}}
\> \hat{=} \>
\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} \sqrt{1 - \frac{C}{L}R^2}
\approx
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}
\newline &
\stackrel{gravitational}{=}
\sqrt{\frac{g}{r}}
\end{split} \tag{17}\]


6.3 Phase:
\[\begin{split} &
\varphi = k \left( \vec{r}\cdot\vec{k} \right) - \omega t
\coloneqq
k s - \omega t = ...
\end{split} \tag{18}\]


6.4 Refraction:
Refraction \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\exists\) Matter; refraction causes dispersion \[\begin{split} &
\eta = \frac{\tan\alpha}{\tan\beta}
\> \hat{=} \>
\mathscr{E}_\beta^{-1} \mathscr{E}_\alpha =
\frac{\varepsilon_\alpha}{\varepsilon_\beta} = ...
\end{split} \tag{19}\]


6.5 Dispersion:
Dispersion \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\exists\) Matter (dielctric madium or conductor); Dispersion \(\neq\) Dissipation \[\begin{split} &
...
\end{split} \tag{20}\]


6.6 Dielectricity:
\[\begin{split} &
\varepsilon = ...
\end{split} \tag{21}\]


6.7 Permeability:
\[\begin{split} &
\mu = ...
\end{split} \tag{22}\]


6.8 Conductivity:
\[\begin{split} &
\sigma = ...
\end{split} \tag{23}\]


6.9 Conductor (charge conductive element for energy transfer) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\sigma \cdot t \gg \varepsilon_0
\end{split} \tag{24}\]


6.10 Slow changing current (quasi-stationary, low frequencies) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\frac{v}{c} \ll \frac{1}{c}\frac{\mathscr{E}}{\mathscr{B}} \ll \frac{c}{v}
\end{split} \tag{25}\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
text:
7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
\[\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( we + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E}
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split} \tag{26}\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 8 Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):
text:
8 Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):
\[\begin{split} &
V =
U_1 - U_2 =
r \mathscr{E} =
\dot{\Psi} + R I + \frac{I}{C}t =
L \dot{I} + R I + \frac{Q}{C}
\end{split} \tag{27}\]
\[
\Delta \cdot \coloneqq \textnormal{ div grad = Laplacian}
\]\[  
J = \textnormal{"ignition of inhomogenity,cause"} \quad
j = \textnormal{Current density} \quad
\varrho = \textnormal{Charge density}
\]\[  
V = \textnormal{Potentialdifference} \quad  
I = \textnormal{Current} \quad
R = \textnormal{Resistance} \quad
\]\[  
L = \textnormal{Inductance} \qquad  
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
Q = c\textnormal{harge (ion)} \qquad
\Psi = LI = \textnormal{mag. Flux}
\]\[
\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} = \textnormal{Poynting-Vector = radiation intensity}
\]\[
D_S = \int \left( \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} \right) \, dr = \int \textnormal{div}(\mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H}) \, dr = \textnormal{dissipation engergy lost from radiation} \approx \textnormal{0 for low frequencies } \omega
\]\[
d = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{\omega}} = \textnormal{skin depth (surface) of conductor} \qquad
r = \frac{d}{\sqrt{2i}}\rho = \frac{d}{1+i}\rho = \textnormal{radius (extension) of conductor}
\]\[
\rho = kr = \textnormal{"radius of curvature $k$"}
\]\[
\mu = \textnormal{permeability of conductor}    \qquad
\sigma = \textnormal{conductivity of conductor (dielectricum)} \qquad
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
9 Low Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \ll \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \ll d \iff \rho \ll 1 \end{split} \tag{28}\] \[
L \approx const.    \qquad
C \approx const.    \qquad
D_S \approx 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
10 High Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \gg \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \gg d \iff \rho \gg 1 \end{split} \tag{29}\] \[
L(t) \neq const.    \qquad
C(t) \neq const.   \qquad
\textnormal{for Dipol: } D_S \propto \omega^4 \gg 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
11 Continuity at Rest: Electic Charge
no Dissipation, no velocity, charge (ion) causes Electric Field

title: PHYSECON
text:
12 Continuity at Movement: Magnetic Field
no Dissipation, Velocity constant, moved charge (ion) causes emergence of Magnetic Field

title: PHYSECON
text:
13 Continuity at Acceleration: Radiation Energy
no Dissipation, Velocity changing, acceleration of charge (ion) causes emgergence of Radiation

title: PHYSECON
section: 14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
text:
14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
\[
2\pi = k \lambda \gg kr_0 \ll 1
\]\[
\omega = kc \ll \frac{c}{r_0}     \qquad
\nabla \cdot j = i \omega \varrho     \qquad
i \omega Q = 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
text:
15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
Refraction index emerges
Dispersion: \(k^2 = \varepsilon \mu \omega^2 + i \mu \sigma \omega\)

title: PHYSECON
text:
16 Near Field
\[
r \ll \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \ll 2\pi
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} \sim \frac{ \frac{\vec{p}^2}{\varepsilon_0 r^6} }{ \frac{k^2 \mu_0 c^2 \vec{p}^2}{r^4} } \sim \left( kr \right)^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\vec{p} = \textnormal{elec. Dipol}
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
17 Far Field
accelerated movement -> Radiation in Far Field of charge source
\[
r \gg \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \gg 1
\qquad
r \gg kr_0^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} =
\frac{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 }{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 } = 1
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
18 Dissipation in Vacuum
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter, no Refraction index, Reflection

title: PHYSECON
text:
19 Dissipation in Matter
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter with Dielectricum, Refraction merges, Reflection, Absorbtion

title: PHYSECON
text:
20 Energy gain by excitation:
Action Quantum, Planck Constant (Energy-Frequency-Slope): 
\[\begin{split} &
h =
\tan(\alpha) =
\frac{E_{kin}}{\nu_i - \nu_0} =
\frac{eV_{0i}}{\nu_i - \nu_0}
\equiv \\&
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\nu_0} =
\frac{W}{\nu_0} =
\frac{F_{EM} \cdot x}{\nu_0} =
\hbar k \lambda
\end{split} \tag{30}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\hbar =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\omega} =
\end{split} \tag{31}\]
\[\begin{split} &
E_{kin}^{max} =
eV_0 =
e \frac{Q}{C} =
h(\nu - \nu_0)
\end{split} \tag{32}\]
\[
V_0 = \textnormal{Opposing Potential ($I=0$)}
\]\[
\alpha = \angle( E_{kin}, \nu ) \qquad
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
Q = \textnormal{ion charge} \qquad
\]\[
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
\nu = \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} \sqrt{1 - \frac{C}{L}R^2}
\]\[
\Rightarrow \textnormal{Inductance } L = \frac{4\pi^2}{C} \left( \frac{e}{h}Q + \nu_0 \right)^2
\]


\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Ionisation Energy of chemical element } E_i = h\nu = h\frac{\lambda}{c} = \hbar\omega = \frac{h}{k\lambda} \omega\)
\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Energy released (gained) } E_{gain} = E_{pot} - E_i = mc^2 - h\nu\)

text: About this site

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text: WORKING NOTES DRAFT

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.1 Potential as “intrinsic energy of inertia”

1.1.1 Electro-Magnetic Field (force caused by potential)

static: lovslized source charge at rest, only spacial dependency, no time dependency
stationary: source charce at uniform movement, steady current flow, space and time dependency
dynamic: source charge at fast movement, radiation, space and time dependency
relativistc: source charge at very speedy movement, radiation, space and time dependency




rest localization vs. time-dependency
slow vs. fast movemnt
vacuum vs. matter
local vs. global of \(\mathscr{B}\), singularity vs. regularity of \(\mathscr{E}\)
macro vs. micro
far-field vs. near-field




1.1.1.1 Magnetic Field in Vacuum
\[\begin{split} &
\mathscr{B} = \nabla \times \boldsymbol{U_m} \coloneqq rot(A)
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Matter}}{=}
\mu_0 \left( \mathscr{H} + \mathscr{M} \right)
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Vacuum}}{=}
\frac{1}{c} \left( \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \times \mathscr{E} \right)
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Far Field}}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \mu_0 \frac{k^2 e^{i(kr - \omega t)}}{r} \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \times \left( c p_{0_{dipol}} + m_{0_{dipol}} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Near Field}}{\approx}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \mu_0 q \frac{v \times r}{\left| r \right|^2}
\approx
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} i k c \mu_0 \frac{ \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \times p_m}{r^2}
\end{split} \tag{1}\]


1.1.1.2 Magnetic Field in Matter
Exitation \(\hat{=}\) Displacement \[\begin{split} &
\text{Exication:} \qquad
\mathscr{H} = \frac{1}{\mu_0}\mathscr{B} - \mathscr{M} = \frac{1}{\mu}\mathscr{B}
\newline &
\text{Magnetisation:} \qquad
\mathscr{M} = \frac{\mu - \mu_0}{\mu\mu_0}\mathscr{B} = \frac{\mu - \mu_0}{\mu_0}\mathscr{H}
\end{split} \tag{2}\]


1.1.1.3 Electric Field in Vacuum
\[\begin{split} &
\mathscr{E}
\stackrel{\textnormal{conservativ}}{=}
-\nabla U_e
\stackrel{\textnormal{dynamic}}{=}
-\nabla U_e - \frac{\partial}{\partial t} \boldsymbol{U_m}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{fast moving charge}}{=}
\newline &
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \frac{1}{r} \frac{1}{\left( 1 - \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \cdot \frac{v}{c} \right)^3 } q
\left[
\underbrace{
\left( 1 - \frac{v^2}{c^2} \right) \frac{\frac{r}{\left| r \right|} - \frac{v}{c}}{r}
}_\text{uniform movement}
+
\underbrace{
    \frac{\frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \times \left[ \left( \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} - \frac{v}{c} \right) \times \dot{v} \right]
}{c^2}
}_\text{accelerated movement}
\right]
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Far Field}}
= c \mathscr{B} \times \frac{1}{\left| r \right|}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{Near Field}}
\approx
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} q \frac{r}{\left| r \right|^3}
\approx
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \frac{ 3 \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \cdot p_m \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} - p_m}{r^3}
\end{split} \tag{3}\]


1.1.1.4 Electric Field in Matter
Displacement \(\hat{=}\) Exitation (field change)
dielectricum = non-conducting material
electricum = conductor medium
\[\begin{split} &
\text{Exication:} \qquad
\mathscr{D} = \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E} + \mathscr{P} =
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{isotropic medium in weak fields}}{=}
\newline &
\varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E} + \varepsilon_0 \mbox{\Large$\chi$} \mathscr{E} =
\varepsilon_0 \left( 1 + \mbox{\Large$\chi$}  \right) \mathscr{E} =
\newline &
\varepsilon_0 4\pi r^3 n \left< p_m \right> =
4\pi r^3 n \varepsilon_0 \left< \mathscr{E}_m \right> =
\alpha n \varepsilon_0 \bar{\mathscr{E}}_m =
\varepsilon \mathscr{E}
\newline &
\text{Polarisation:} \qquad
\mathscr{P} = \left( \varepsilon - \varepsilon_0 \right) \mathscr{E}
\end{split} \tag{4}\]


1.1.1.5 Abbreviations
\[
\Phi = \text{electric potential} \qquad
A = \text{magnetic potential} \qquad
j = \nabla \times \mathscr{H} = \text{elec. current density}
\]\[
\mathscr{E} = \text{Electric Field} \qquad
\mathscr{B} = \text{Magnetic Field}     \qquad      \nabla \cdot \mathscr{B} = 0    \qquad
\mathscr{D} = \text{Displacement density of dielectricum} \qquad
\mathscr{M} = \text{magnetisation density}
\]\[
\mathscr{P} = \text{Polarisation} \qquad
\mathscr{H} = \text{magn. exitation, magn. field strength ("magn. displacement")}  \qquad
\]\[
n = \text{spacial molecule density in volume}
\]\[
\alpha = 4\pi r^3 = constant     \qquad
\mbox{\Large$\chi$} = \text{elec. susceptibility of dieelectricum}    \qquad
\kappa = \frac{\mu - \mu_0}{\mu_0} = \text{magn. susceptibility}
\]\[
\left< p_m \right> = \text{spacial average of magn. dipol moment} \qquad
\left< \mathscr{E}_m \right> = \text{spacial average of electric field}  
\]\[
\bar{\mathscr{E}}_m = \text{time average of electric field} \qquad
\mu = \text{magn. permeability} \qquad
\mu_0 = \text{magn. constant ...}
\]\[
\varepsilon = \text{dielectricity constant} \qquad
\varepsilon_0 = \text{...}
\]



1.1.2 Electro-Magnetic interactions/coupling (Maxwell conditions)

1.1.2.1 conservativ, static charge localisation:
…


1.1.2.2 stationary, slow charge movement:
\[\begin{split} &
\nabla \cdot \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E} = \varrho \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad
\nabla \cdot \mathscr{B} = 0
\newline &
\nabla \times \mathscr{E} = -\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \mathscr{B} = i\omega\mathscr{B}  \qquad
\nabla \times \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B} = j + \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial}{\partial t} \mathscr{E} = j - i\omega\varepsilon_0\mathscr{E}
\end{split} \tag{5}\]


1.1.2.3 relativistic, fast charge movement:
…

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.2 Potential
\(\Rightarrow\) Wave equation that determines Potential: \[\begin{split} &
\Delta \cdot U - \frac{1}{c^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} U = J
\end{split} \tag{6}\]
\(\Rightarrow\) Potential caused by “ignition J” (inhomgenity) from source at rest, v = 0: \[\begin{split} &
U = -\frac{1}{4\pi} \int \frac{J}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\end{split} \tag{7}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.3 elec. Potential structure:
\(J = -\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \varrho\) \[\begin{split} &
U_e \coloneqq \Phi
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. charge dens.=}\varrho}{=}
\frac{1}{4\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \int \frac{\varrho}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} Q =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} It
\end{split} \tag{8}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.4 magn. Potential structure:
\(J = -\mu_0 j\) \[\begin{split} &
U_m \coloneqq \boldsymbol{A}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. current dens.=}j}{=}
\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \int \frac{j}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \mu_0 I =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} I =
\frac{1}{c^2} \frac{1}{t} U_e
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{source in uniformal movement,} v = const. \neq 0}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \mu_0 Qv
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} vQ
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Itv =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Ir
\end{split} \tag{9}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.5 Electric endogen energy

Bond Energy
Binding Energy

\(E_{pot} = mc^2\)
\[\begin{split} &
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
q = ne = \textnormal{charge} \qquad
n \in \mathbb{N}
\end{split} \tag{10}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\textnormal{Potential} \qquad
U =
\frac{E_{pot}}{q} =
\frac{\left( mc^2 \right)^2}{ne} =
\frac{m}{q} m c^4 =
\kappa m c^4
\end{split} \tag{11}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.6 Electric induced energy (exogenous excitation)

Ionisation Energy
Exitation Energy

\(E_{kin} = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \> \hat{=} \> \left( pc \right)^2 \> \hat{=} \> h\nu\)
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density electric: \[\begin{split} &
w_e = \frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E}^2
\end{split} \tag{12}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density magnetic: \[\begin{split} &
w_m = \frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B}^2
\end{split} \tag{13}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Electric Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_e = \frac{1}{2} CU^2
\end{split} \tag{14}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Magnetic Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_m = \frac{1}{2} LI^2
\end{split} \tag{15}\]

1.6.1 Power:
\[\begin{split} &
P = VI = (U_1 - U_2) \frac{Q}{t} =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{t} = \frac{W}{t} = \frac{Fr}{t} = Fv
\end{split} \tag{16}\]


1.6.2 Frequency:
\[\begin{split} &
\omega = \dot{\rho} = \frac{v}{r} = 2 \pi \nu = k \lambda \frac{1}{T}
\stackrel{vacuum}{=}
kc
\newline &
\stackrel{matter}{=}
k \frac{c}{\eta} = kc \frac{\tan\beta}{\tan\alpha}
\> \hat{=} \>
k c \left( \mathscr{E_\beta}^{-1} \mathscr{E_\alpha} \right)^{-1} =
k c \frac{\varepsilon_\beta}{\varepsilon_\alpha} =
k c \sqrt{\frac{\varepsilon_0\mu_0}{\varepsilon\mu}}
\> \hat{=} \>
\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} \sqrt{1 - \frac{C}{L}R^2}
\approx
\frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}
\newline &
\stackrel{gravitational}{=}
\sqrt{\frac{g}{r}}
\end{split} \tag{17}\]


1.6.3 Phase:
\[\begin{split} &
\varphi = k \left( \vec{r}\cdot\vec{k} \right) - \omega t
\coloneqq
k s - \omega t = ...
\end{split} \tag{18}\]


1.6.4 Refraction:
Refraction \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\exists\) Matter; refraction causes dispersion \[\begin{split} &
\eta = \frac{\tan\alpha}{\tan\beta}
\> \hat{=} \>
\mathscr{E}_\beta^{-1} \mathscr{E}_\alpha =
\frac{\varepsilon_\alpha}{\varepsilon_\beta} = ...
\end{split} \tag{19}\]


1.6.5 Dispersion:
Dispersion \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\exists\) Matter (dielctric madium or conductor); Dispersion \(\neq\) Dissipation \[\begin{split} &
...
\end{split} \tag{20}\]


1.6.6 Dielectricity:
\[\begin{split} &
\varepsilon = ...
\end{split} \tag{21}\]


1.6.7 Permeability:
\[\begin{split} &
\mu = ...
\end{split} \tag{22}\]


1.6.8 Conductivity:
\[\begin{split} &
\sigma = ...
\end{split} \tag{23}\]


1.6.9 Conductor (charge conductive element for energy transfer) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\sigma \cdot t \gg \varepsilon_0
\end{split} \tag{24}\]


1.6.10 Slow changing current (quasi-stationary, low frequencies) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\frac{v}{c} \ll \frac{1}{c}\frac{\mathscr{E}}{\mathscr{B}} \ll \frac{c}{v}
\end{split} \tag{25}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
section: 1.7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
text:
1.7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
\[\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( we + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E}
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split} \tag{26}\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
section: 1.8 Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):
text:
1.8 Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):
\[\begin{split} &
V =
U_1 - U_2 =
r \mathscr{E} =
\dot{\Psi} + R I + \frac{I}{C}t =
L \dot{I} + R I + \frac{Q}{C}
\end{split} \tag{27}\]
\[
\Delta \cdot \coloneqq \textnormal{ div grad = Laplacian}
\]\[  
J = \textnormal{"ignition of inhomogenity,cause"} \quad
j = \textnormal{Current density} \quad
\varrho = \textnormal{Charge density}
\]\[  
V = \textnormal{Potentialdifference} \quad  
I = \textnormal{Current} \quad
R = \textnormal{Resistance} \quad
\]\[  
L = \textnormal{Inductance} \qquad  
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
Q = c\textnormal{harge (ion)} \qquad
\Psi = LI = \textnormal{mag. Flux}
\]\[
\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} = \textnormal{Poynting-Vector = radiation intensity}
\]\[
D_S = \int \left( \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} \right) \, dr = \int \textnormal{div}(\mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H}) \, dr = \textnormal{dissipation engergy lost from radiation} \approx \textnormal{0 for low frequencies } \omega
\]\[
d = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{\omega}} = \textnormal{skin depth (surface) of conductor} \qquad
r = \frac{d}{\sqrt{2i}}\rho = \frac{d}{1+i}\rho = \textnormal{radius (extension) of conductor}
\]\[
\rho = kr = \textnormal{"radius of curvature $k$"}
\]\[
\mu = \textnormal{permeability of conductor}    \qquad
\sigma = \textnormal{conductivity of conductor (dielectricum)} \qquad
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.9 Low Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \ll \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \ll d \iff \rho \ll 1 \end{split} \tag{28}\] \[
L \approx const.    \qquad
C \approx const.    \qquad
D_S \approx 0
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.10 High Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \gg \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \gg d \iff \rho \gg 1 \end{split} \tag{29}\] \[
L(t) \neq const.    \qquad
C(t) \neq const.   \qquad
\textnormal{for Dipol: } D_S \propto \omega^4 \gg 0
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.11 Continuity at Rest: Electic Charge
no Dissipation, no velocity, charge (ion) causes Electric Field

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.12 Continuity at Movement: Magnetic Field
no Dissipation, Velocity constant, moved charge (ion) causes emergence of Magnetic Field

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.13 Continuity at Acceleration: Radiation Energy
no Dissipation, Velocity changing, acceleration of charge (ion) causes emgergence of Radiation

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
section: 1.14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
text:
1.14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
\[
2\pi = k \lambda \gg kr_0 \ll 1
\]\[
\omega = kc \ll \frac{c}{r_0}     \qquad
\nabla \cdot j = i \omega \varrho     \qquad
i \omega Q = 0
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
section: 1.15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
text:
1.15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
Refraction index emerges
Dispersion: \(k^2 = \varepsilon \mu \omega^2 + i \mu \sigma \omega\)

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.16 Near Field
\[
r \ll \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \ll 2\pi
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} \sim \frac{ \frac{\vec{p}^2}{\varepsilon_0 r^6} }{ \frac{k^2 \mu_0 c^2 \vec{p}^2}{r^4} } \sim \left( kr \right)^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\vec{p} = \textnormal{elec. Dipol}
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.17 Far Field
accelerated movement -> Radiation in Far Field of charge source
\[
r \gg \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \gg 1
\qquad
r \gg kr_0^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} =
\frac{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 }{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 } = 1
\]

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.18 Dissipation in Vacuum
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter, no Refraction index, Reflection

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.19 Dissipation in Matter
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter with Dielectricum, Refraction merges, Reflection, Absorbtion

title: Physics Concepts as Indicative Framework to Model Systems of Economies Ex-ante
text:
1.20 Energy gain by excitation:
Action Quantum, Planck Constant (Energy-Frequency-Slope): 
\[\begin{split} &
h =
\tan(\alpha) =
\frac{E_{kin}}{\nu_i - \nu_0} =
\frac{eV_{0i}}{\nu_i - \nu_0}
\equiv \\&
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\nu_0} =
\frac{W}{\nu_0} =
\frac{F_{EM} \cdot x}{\nu_0} =
\hbar k \lambda
\end{split} \tag{30}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\hbar =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\omega} =
\end{split} \tag{31}\]
\[\begin{split} &
E_{kin}^{max} =
eV_0 =
e \frac{Q}{C} =
h(\nu - \nu_0)
\end{split} \tag{32}\]
\[
V_0 = \textnormal{Opposing Potential ($I=0$)}
\]\[
\alpha = \angle( E_{kin}, \nu ) \qquad
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
Q = \textnormal{ion charge} \qquad
\]\[
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
\nu = \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}} \sqrt{1 - \frac{C}{L}R^2}
\]\[
\Rightarrow \textnormal{Inductance } L = \frac{4\pi^2}{C} \left( \frac{e}{h}Q + \nu_0 \right)^2
\]


\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Ionisation Energy of chemical element } E_i = h\nu = h\frac{\lambda}{c} = \hbar\omega = \frac{h}{k\lambda} \omega\)
\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Energy released (gained) } E_{gain} = E_{pot} - E_i = mc^2 - h\nu\)
